title: Crate

jokes.text: Let's take our shirts off and work up a sweat.
jokes.setup: Thornhill giving what should be a serious victory speech
jokes.punchline: Random nonsensical statement about removing shirts
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur that establishes Thornhill's incoherent speaking style

jokes.text: Listen to this idiot. They're not even sound bites. They're just sounds.
jokes.punchline: Sound bites vs. just sounds - clever wordplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures the meaninglessness of political speech

jokes.text: I mean, he cornholed us in Iowa.
jokes.punchline: Vulgar metaphor from buttoned-up political operative
jokes.explanation: Crude language creates character comedy through contrast with professional setting

jokes.text: Well, that's appropriate. It's the corn state.
jokes.punchline: Iowa corn connection
jokes.explanation: Quick wit building on previous vulgar comment with geographical pun

jokes.text: And please downplay them so I don't cry.
jokes.punchline: Openly admitting emotional fragility
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's vulnerability and narcissism

jokes.text: She is downplaying them, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Selina asks Amy to downplay bad numbers
jokes.punchline: Mike's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Mike's oblivious literalness contrasts with Selina's desperation

jokes.text: I'm normal. I can be so folksy. Doing the folksy thing with... folks.
jokes.setup: Need to appear relatable to voters
jokes.punchline: Selina's complete inability to sound natural
jokes.explanation: Brilliant demonstration of how fake Selina sounds when trying to be authentic

jokes.text: Remember when we only had five days left? Those were good times. Great times.
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic for being slightly less doomed
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic perspective on their deteriorating situation

jokes.text: It's January, Mike.
jokes.setup: Mike suggests throwing first pitch at ball game
jokes.punchline: Simple factual response highlighting Mike's stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that makes Mike look idiotic with minimal effort

jokes.text: Yeah, and I can't throw a ball, Mike. I'll look like Gary here.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she can't throw first pitch
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to Gary's presumed incompetence
jokes.explanation: Casual insult to Gary shows Selina's thoughtless cruelty

jokes.text: Thank you.
jokes.punchline: Gary's polite thanks for being insulted
jokes.explanation: Gary's pathetic gratitude for abuse perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: Great idea. Talk some real vote meat. Rub with a schlub. That kind of thing.
jokes.punchline: Vote meat/meat metaphor and schlub rhyme
jokes.explanation: Dan's cynical wordplay about voters as meat to be consumed

jokes.text: That's a good point. Nothing says 'regular American' like standing on a street corner shouting at strangers.
jokes.setup: Suggestion about getting on the stump
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the absurdity of campaign stumping
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about the inherent weirdness of political campaigning

jokes.text: What, are you David Coppercock?
jokes.setup: Gary doing magic with attention-getting announcement
jokes.punchline: David Copperfield becomes Coppercock
jokes.explanation: Typical Selina crude wordplay, juvenile but fits her character

jokes.text: Wow, it's a box inside of a box. Jesus! Oh, it's a crate.
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic discovery that it's just a crate
jokes.explanation: Deflation humor as elaborate reveal produces mundane object

jokes.text: I love it. Are you okay?
jokes.setup: Gary apparently hurt himself during crate reveal
jokes.punchline: Selina's delayed, perfunctory concern
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina prioritizing the object over Gary's wellbeing

jokes.text: Okay, for two grand, you don't get a call from me. Okay? Just send him a button or something.
jokes.punchline: Selina's transactional view of campaign donors
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's shallow priorities

jokes.text: You know, they accuse you of not thinking outside the box, but I do think outside the box. You know what else I do? I stand on top of a box.
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of thinking outside the box
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that actually works as both joke and campaign rhetoric

jokes.text: I don't blame her. Imagine being married to that guy. I wouldn't put up with that shit for a second.
jokes.punchline: Selina's complete lack of self-awareness about her own marriage
jokes.explanation: Rich irony given Selina's own failed marriage and difficult personality

jokes.text: Do you want to... No, I like standing up here and looking down at everybody.
jokes.setup: Offered chance to step down from crate
jokes.punchline: Selina enjoying the literal and metaphorical elevation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character insight into Selina's need for superiority and control

jokes.text: I want to hear what you see. And I wanna see what you feel.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to sound profound and connected to voters
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical mixing of senses
jokes.explanation: Typical political gibberish that sounds meaningful but is nonsense

jokes.text: because if I don't communicate, guys, I communi-can't.
jokes.punchline: Forced wordplay with communicate/can't
jokes.explanation: Cringeworthy political pun that shows Selina's desperation to be clever

jokes.text: Seriously, you do me better than I do me. You do yourself a lot.
jokes.punchline: Accidental sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Quick back-and-forth with inadvertent masturbation reference

jokes.text: It's like a chicken came in your mouth.
jokes.punchline: Grossly inappropriate sexual metaphor for food
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah - inappropriate sexual comment that makes everyone uncomfortable

jokes.text: People can smell the stench of death and they're running for the lifeboats. Really? Women and tall, talented men first.
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'women and children first' with Dan's specific concerns
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on familiar phrase that reveals Dan's vanity and priorities

jokes.text: You've got no skills. Your uncle controls a lot of votes, sure. But you? If you tried to clap, you'd miss your hands.
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of Jonah being too incompetent to clap
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating insult that creates vivid, absurd image of incompetence

jokes.text: Boom, Isabella Herrera. Boom, Liz Graham. Ace up the sleeve... booya... Ally Craig.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic presentation of made-up names
jokes.explanation: Good escalating performance showing Jonah's pathetic attempts at seeming important

jokes.text: Take your crazy eggs, stick 'em up your ass.
jokes.punchline: Incorporating Jonah's weird egg order into dismissal
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the egg conversation in the form of an insult

jokes.text: Meanwhile, I can now confidently spread the story that the Maddox camp is hemorrhaging like a burst dog.
jokes.punchline: Grotesque metaphor of burst dog
jokes.explanation: Dan's casual cruelty expressed through disturbing imagery

jokes.text: Just try to make it look like a regular crate, Gary, not Thor's hammer.
jokes.setup: Gary struggling with the heavy crate
jokes.punchline: Comparison to mythical hammer only worthy can lift
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that highlights both the crate's weight and Gary's weakness

jokes.text: Oh, God. That Dickensian shitstack?
jokes.punchline: Combining literary reference with vulgar insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina combination of pretension and vulgarity in one phrase

jokes.text: Crate Expectations.
jokes.setup: Naming the article about Selina's crate
jokes.punchline: Play on Dickens novel 'Great Expectations'
jokes.explanation: Clever literary pun that works for the situation, shows Carter's pretentiousness

jokes.text: She thinks it looks folksy, but she really looks like Dustin Hoffman on a crate.
jokes.setup: Mike describing Selina on the crate
jokes.punchline: Unflattering comparison to short actor
jokes.explanation: Visual joke about Selina's height and appearance, surprisingly sharp from Mike

jokes.text: I gotta go make noises out of my face hole or whatever it is I do.
jokes.punchline: Reducing press secretary role to basic biological function
jokes.explanation: Mike's self-awareness about his incompetence expressed through crude self-description

jokes.text: If I could lift the fucking crate, I'd beat him to death with it.
jokes.punchline: Violent fantasy thwarted by crate's weight
jokes.explanation: Violent fantasy made funnier by practical limitation, classic Selina rage

jokes.text: Otherwise she's gonna hang herself from the nearest light fixture.
jokes.setup: Joking about giving FLOTUS a crate
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to suicide method
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about suicide that shows Selina's callousness

jokes.text: They all call me a dick behind my back. But, like, right behind my back so I can hear them.
jokes.punchline: People don't even bother to hide their contempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - pathetic self-awareness about how much he's disliked

jokes.text: God, I wish I had a dirty bomb.
jokes.punchline: Casual terrorism reference
jokes.explanation: Jonah's casual extremism shows his disconnect from reality

jokes.text: You don't have to fuck him, Mom. You just have to call him.
jokes.setup: Mom reluctant to call Uncle Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jonah's crude clarification
jokes.explanation: Jonah's inappropriate language with his mother shows his social cluelessness

jokes.text: Most people say Hitler.
jokes.setup: Selina trying to avoid naming someone she despises
jokes.punchline: Carter's matter-of-fact revelation
jokes.explanation: Reveals how obviously bad Selina's non-answer was

jokes.text: So, change it to Hitler?
jokes.punchline: Selina's eager adaptation to the expected answer
jokes.explanation: Selina's shameless pivot to the popular answer shows her lack of principles

jokes.text: Oh, God, yes, you can... be.
jokes.punchline: Mike's involuntary agreement before catching himself
jokes.explanation: Mike accidentally confirming Selina's worst trait before trying to recover

jokes.text: Neil Diamond gave the same answer.
jokes.punchline: Comparing VP to aging pop star
jokes.explanation: Absurd comparison that deflates political importance with random celebrity

jokes.text: Siri, why does God allow suffering?
jokes.punchline: Asking Siri instead of engaging with the question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern absurdist moment - deep question reduced to voice assistant query

jokes.text: He has his head so up his ass, he can wave out his mouth.
jokes.punchline: Extreme anatomical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of common phrase into absurd visual image, peak Selina insult

jokes.text: GUMMI... Give Us More Money, Idiot.
jokes.punchline: Acronym that sounds innocent but is insulting
jokes.explanation: Clever acronym joke that reveals campaign's cynical view of donors

jokes.text: I always call them dicks. It doesn't stand for anything.
jokes.punchline: Dan's straightforward approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan line - cutting through bureaucratic nonsense with simple insult

jokes.text: Delete it. It knows too much.
jokes.punchline: Treating phone like sentient threat
jokes.explanation: Spy movie logic applied to mundane phone situation

jokes.text: You cannot be serious.
jokes.setup: Trying to guess Carter's phone password
jokes.punchline: Dan's exasperation at obvious password
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction to predictable password, functional but not special

jokes.text: Don't know. Birmingham. Have you heard of Birmingham?
jokes.setup: Gary stalling Carter with small talk
jokes.punchline: Gary's social awkwardness in asking obvious question
jokes.explanation: Gary's painful small talk attempts show his social incompetence

jokes.text: No, the light bulb's fine, see?
jokes.setup: Quincy discovering them with his phone
jokes.punchline: Absurd cover story about lightbulb inspection
jokes.explanation: Decent improvised lie humor showing quick thinking under pressure

jokes.text: Exit, pursued by a bear.
jokes.punchline: Shakespeare reference as exit line
jokes.explanation: Pretentious Shakespeare quote fits Carter's affected character

jokes.text: A bit of a bitch. Be a little feisty.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic correction to softer term
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jonah's fake politeness and self-awareness

jokes.text: She does drugs. She's got that tattoo...
jokes.punchline: Listing Lucy's supposed transgressions
jokes.explanation: Family dysfunction showing where Jonah gets his issues

jokes.text: How do you not just punch yourself in the face?
jokes.punchline: Extreme expression of exasperation
jokes.explanation: Jonah's hyperbolic reaction showing his own self-loathing projected outward

jokes.text: How do you not just punch yourself in the face?
jokes.punchline: Suggesting self-violence as logical response
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Jonah's abusive relationship with his mother

jokes.text: may want to reflect on how its tightfisted idiocy is mocked by the woman on the $1,200 crate.
jokes.punchline: Formal language describing campaign disaster
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of crate disaster with formal journalism language contrasting with absurdity

jokes.text: You know what VP stands for? It stands for 'Victory Permafucked.'
jokes.punchline: Redefining VP acronym to reflect hopelessness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark redefinition that perfectly captures Selina's despair and vulgarity

jokes.text: Every motherfucking one of you. Loser! Loser!
jokes.punchline: Complete meltdown blaming her staff
jokes.explanation: Selina's complete breakdown showing her true character under pressure

jokes.text: Take your lithium, jag-offs.
jokes.punchline: Amy's dismissive mental health insult
jokes.explanation: Amy's crude dismissal of press questions, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Let's find us a house, Wendy, where we can keep ducks. For us and the ducks.
jokes.punchline: Specific fantasy about domestic life with ducks
jokes.explanation: Mike's oddly specific escape fantasy shows his complete disconnect from reality

jokes.text: sometimes through no fault of your own, it's just one... disaster after another disaster.
jokes.punchline: Describing her own campaign while talking to actual war victims
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Selina's complete self-absorption while talking to actual tragedy victims

jokes.text: Kind of, in a way, and also not. And in a third way both.
jokes.punchline: Incomprehensibly analytical response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent character moment - over-analytical response that makes no practical sense

jokes.text: Ma'am, we're America. We're always at war.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if they're at war
jokes.punchline: Kent's matter-of-fact response about American foreign policy
jokes.explanation: Kent's clinical observation about perpetual warfare is both funny and uncomfortably true

jokes.text: Where is he... Yeah, 'cause doesn't he have to move? Well, yeah, but does he have a house?
jokes.punchline: Gary's focus on mundane housing logistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - missing the historical significance for practical details

jokes.text: When I get excited, my nose bleeds!
jokes.setup: Gary's nose starts bleeding during excitement
jokes.punchline: Bizarre physical response to emotion
jokes.explanation: Weird character detail that makes Gary even more pathetic and unusual

jokes.text: Why is there a bicycle book?
jokes.setup: Looking through Gary's bag for tissues
jokes.punchline: Discovering Gary's random hobby
jokes.explanation: Reveals another layer of Gary's odd personal life

jokes.text: I was gonna raise ducks.
jokes.setup: Learning he has to stay for presidency
jokes.punchline: Mike's disappointed duck farming dreams
jokes.explanation: Callback to Mike's earlier escape fantasy, showing his priorities

jokes.text: gosh, good luck with all of your terrible troubles and, um, welcome to America.
jokes.punchline: Selina's awkward, insensitive farewell
jokes.explanation: Selina's tone-deaf dismissal of serious refugee trauma

jokes.text: That's what all the chicks say.
jokes.setup: Selina saying Ben has to come with her
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo response to professional statement
jokes.explanation: Standard Ben crudeness, fits character but predictable sexual innuendo

jokes.text: This is better than any of the sex I've ever had. I mean, almost any of it.
jokes.punchline: Sexual comparison with self-correction
jokes.explanation: Dan's crude enthusiasm with typical sexual reference and hedge

jokes.text: Someone has just flown two planes into my career.
jokes.setup: Learning about Selina's promotion while he's jobless
jokes.punchline: 9/11 metaphor for career destruction
jokes.explanation: Dark metaphor that's typical Jonah inappropriate historical reference

jokes.text: Buy yourself a gun, Mom, because America's gonna have to shoot its way out of this.
jokes.setup: Jonah's reaction to Selina becoming president
jokes.punchline: Extreme apocalyptic prediction requiring violence
jokes.explanation: Jonah's typical extreme overreaction to political news

jokes.text: I'm gonna be the fucking president.
jokes.punchline: Selina's profane realization of her achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending capturing Selina's disbelief and vulgarity at historic moment

tmdb_overview: With Thornhill ahead in the polls, Selina and her staff discuss how to make her more "folksy." In need of a job, Jonah meets with Bill Ericsson. Selina has an interview with pretentious journalist Quincy Carter.
insight: Wordplay carries a middling episode as cringe takes backseat to character comedy.
